title: After a devastating fire, South End condo owners say over $800,000 went missing
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: South End condo trustees allege Premier Property Solutions stole over $800,000 meant for fire restoration.
Trustees discovered missing funds during efforts to pay contractors for the ongoing construction in May 2026.
Premier Property Solutions transitioned management to Patrick Colbert, who oversaw the accounts with the trust.

title: Red Sox continue to get bad news on Roman Anthony’s recovery from lingering wrist injury
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony has been sidelined since May 4 due to a sprained right wrist and hand.
Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy reports Anthony has made little progress in his recovery.
Anthony's injury, initially thought to last over a week, involves healing torn ligaments, delaying his return.

title: Celtics select St. John’s wing Dillon Mitchell with 40th pick in 2026 NBA Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Celtics selected St. John's wing Dillon Mitchell with the 40th pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.
Mitchell, a 6-foot-7 wing, earned All-Big East Third Team and All Defensive Team honors at St. John's.
Mitchell, known for his defense, struggled offensively, shooting 48% on free throws and 1 of 15 on threes last season.

title: Boy, 13, hospitalized after being found unresponsive in swimming pool at Beverly home
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 13-year-old boy was found unresponsive in a swimming pool at a Beverly home on Wednesday.
Bystanders and first responders administered CPR, stabilizing the boy before he was flown to a Boston hospital.
The incident is under investigation by Beverly police, following the boy's hospitalization after pool rescue.

title: Here’s where every tall ship will be docked for Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 tall ships will dock across Charlestown, downtown Boston, and the Seaport area during Sail Boston 250.
Notable ships include Italy's Amerigo Vespucci, Argentina's Libertad, and Peru's BAP Unión, berthed in the Seaport District.
Public viewing and boarding are available from July 12-15, with vessels open from 10 a.m. to 10 p.m. daily.

title: Windhorst: ‘Very good chance’ Jaylen Brown isn’t on Celtics next season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is at the center of trade talks as the Celtics consider moving him this offseason.
ESPN's Brian Windhorst suggests there's a strong possibility Jaylen Brown won't be with the Celtics next season.
The Celtics reportedly offered Brown and two first-round picks for Giannis Antetokounmpo, who was traded to Miami.

title: WCVB weeknight meteorologist Mike Wankum will retire end of June, station announces
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mike Wankum, WCVB's weeknight meteorologist for over 20 years, will retire on June 30.
Cindy Fitzgibbon will take over Wankum’s weeknight role; Kelly Ann Cicalese moves to weekdays.
Wankum has 17 regional Emmy Awards and is a Certified Broadcast Meteorologist with the AMS.

title: 14 years later, man charged in death of 7-year-old in Central Mass.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Steven Stuart, 35, of Auburn, is charged with murder in the 2017 death of Jayden Carlson after a 2012 assault.
Stuart was previously convicted in 2015 for assaulting Jayden, leading to severe injuries and years in a nursing home.
Jayden Carlson died from medical complications at age 7; Stuart pleaded not guilty and is held without bail pending trial.

title: Is your favorite Boston bar packed with World Cup revelers? Here are some FIFA-free alternatives.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Grendel’s Den in Cambridge offers a World Cup-free experience with no television for a quiet pub atmosphere.
Blossom Bar at Sichuan Garden in Brookline remains a peaceful retreat, avoiding the influx of FIFA fans.
Wink and Nod in Boston's South End is a speakeasy cocktail lounge without TV, providing respite from World Cup excitement.

title: Man allegedly stole nearly $2,000 from Medford church donation boxes, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Michael Bagley, 60, was arrested for allegedly stealing nearly $2,000 from Medford churches' donation boxes.
Bagley faces charges including larceny, breaking into a depository, and possession of a burglarious instrument.
Video evidence links Bagley to thefts at St. Joseph’s Parish and St. Francis Church dating back to May 19.
